title: Product Manager, Guest Discovery and Homepage
location: San Francisco CA, Los Angeles CA, New York NY
score_rationale: Riley's core expertise in AI-powered search, discovery, personalization, and recommendation systems maps almost directly onto Airbnb's homepage discovery mandate — including hybrid search, ranking, behavioral signals, and A/B experimentation at scale (10M+ monthly users at Samsung). The consumer marketplace discovery archetype and the AI-plus-UX framing are a strong match. The primary gap is the consumer travel/marketplace vertical and the explicit marketing collaboration + product narrative storytelling component, which is less visible in Riley's resume, and the role skews slightly senior (8+ years required, strong exec alignment emphasis) which is solidly within reach but competitive.
recommendation_reason: Deep AI discovery, ranking, and personalization experience directly mirrors the role's core mandate; strong evidence bullets at relevant scale make this a highly competitive application.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years building AI-powered search, discovery, and personalization systems that move the needle for millions of consumers — including hybrid search, ranking, behavioral signals, and recommendation engines at Samsung.com (10M+ monthly users) and Kolon's $2B GMV marketplace. Proven track record of shipping end-to-end discovery experiences by partnering with ML, design, and data science teams and aligning executives behind measurable outcomes.
top_bullets: Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, optimizing user engagement through A/B testing — resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion
Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, increasing web navigation CSAT/NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148→$185) on a $2B GMV platform through iterative A/B testing
gap_handling: The role has a notable emphasis on crafting external product narratives and partnering with marketing/lifecycle campaigns — an area less visible in Riley's resume. Add 1–2 bullets at Samsung or Kolon that reference GTM storytelling, exec presentations, or any cross-channel marketing collaboration (e.g., the conversational AI launch with marketing, or the AEO pipeline announcement). Even a brief mention of "authored product narrative for executive review and global rollout" will close this gap on paper.

title: Senior Product Manager, AI (Customer Agent)
score_rationale: Riley's profile is a strong match on AI/ML product experience — conversational AI, LLM pipelines, search ranking, and recommendation systems all transfer well to Klaviyo's Customer Agent role. However, the role is squarely B2B SaaS (brand-facing configuration, onboarding flows, and agent tooling for business customers), while Riley's primary archetypes are AI Search and E-commerce Discovery — the B2B platform exposure at Amazon is real but narrower than what this role demands. Riley also lacks explicit RAG/agent architecture experience stated on the resume, and has no direct messaging-channel PM background (SMS, RCS, email automation), which is central to Klaviyo's multi-channel agent vision.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and conversational product credentials clear the bar, but limited B2B SaaS onboarding and multi-channel messaging experience creates a meaningful gap that requires targeted resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered and conversational AI experiences — including an end-to-end on-site AI chat agent, hybrid search systems, and personalized recommendation pipelines serving 10M+ users. Proven track record collaborating with ML/data science teams to define agent behavior, build feedback loops, and drive measurable outcomes across complex B2C and B2B product surfaces.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: Riley's resume doesn't explicitly mention RAG, agent architectures, or multi-channel deployment (SMS/RCS/email). Before applying, add a brief callout in the conversational AI bullet noting any retrieval or grounding mechanisms used, and consider adding a line about channel orchestration if the Samsung chat experience touched multiple surfaces. If RAG/LLM infrastructure was involved even peripherally, name it explicitly.

title: Staff Product Manager, Search Algorithms
location: New York City, NY; San Francisco, CA
score_rationale: Riley's experience is a strong domain match — AI-powered search, ranking optimization, relevance metrics, and B2B platform work all align tightly with Faire's core needs. However, the role is titled Staff PM (one level above Riley's target of Senior/PM III), requires 7+ years specifically in complex search systems at high-growth startups, and Faire's context is B2B wholesale marketplace rather than consumer e-commerce, which is a moderate domain shift. The seniority stretch and the hybrid NYC/SF in-office requirement (3 days/week) with no remote option also add friction.
recommendation_reason: Excellent domain alignment on search algorithms and ML, but Staff-level seniority is a stretch and in-office NYC/SF requirement creates location friction for a Bellevue-based candidate.
suggested_summary: Product leader with 9+ years driving AI-powered search and discovery systems at scale, including hybrid search ranking, query understanding, and relevance metric design for 10M+ monthly users. Proven track record partnering with ML engineers and data scientists to ship search algorithm improvements — precision@3 +30%, 1st-result CTR +45% — with deep experience in B2B catalog structures and e-commerce discovery at Amazon and Samsung.
top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, with multi-modal classification pipelines across 20+ high-impact categories
gap_handling: The Staff PM title and 7+ years specifically in search at high-growth startups are the key gaps. In your cover letter and resume summary, explicitly frame your combined Samsung + Amazon tenure as continuous search/discovery system ownership, and emphasize the complexity and scale (10M+ MAU, 2M+ B2B sellers) rather than company size. Lean into the B2B angle from Amazon to bridge to Faire's wholesale marketplace context.

title: Product Manager, API Growth
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials and deep experience with funnel optimization, A/B testing, and growth metrics that map directly to this role's core asks. However, the role is explicitly focused on developer/API platform growth — onboarding, console, docs, billing, self-serve upgrades — and Riley's portfolio skews consumer-side (e-commerce, search, D2C), with no direct experience building for developers or API/platform products. The Amazon B2B tools experience is the closest signal but it was seller-facing, not developer-platform. Anthropic's stature, visa sponsorship, and AI mission alignment are strong pulls for Riley's candidacy.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI credentials and growth fundamentals, but lack of developer-platform or API product experience is a meaningful gap in a role that explicitly requires it — worth applying with a tailored framing.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving measurable growth through search, discovery, and intelligent product experiences at Samsung and Amazon — with a consistent track record in funnel optimization, A/B experimentation, and cross-functional delivery at scale. Passionate about building AI-powered products that create real user value, with hands-on experience partnering with ML engineers and data scientists to ship impactful features for millions of users.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines across 20+ categories
Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, increasing NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users through phased A/B testing across key funnels

Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% through model optimization and iterative A/B testing on a $2B+ GMV platform
gap_handling: Riley has no direct developer-platform or API product experience — the biggest gap here. To bridge it, the resume and cover letter should explicitly reframe the Amazon B2B listing tool as a "platform" build (ML pipelines, structured data, self-serve workflows for 2M+ external users), and the AEO pipeline work at Samsung should be positioned as building API-adjacent infrastructure. In the cover letter, acknowledge the pivot from consumer/e-commerce growth to developer growth and make the case using first-principles: funnel optimization, activation, and monetization mechanics are transferable, and Riley's AI depth is genuine differentiation at an AI-first company.

title: Principal, Product Manager - AI / LLM
score_rationale: Riley's core AI/LLM skills — conversational AI, RAG-adjacent work (AEO pipeline, hybrid search), personalization, and LLM-driven product experience — map strongly to the technical substance of this role. The conversational AI chat experience at Samsung and the AEO metadata pipeline are particularly relevant to Copilot backend and knowledge-layer work. However, this role is titled "Principal PM" and explicitly requires 8+ years focused on backend services, internal platforms, and developer tooling — Riley's background skews toward consumer-facing e-commerce discovery rather than B2B/developer platform infrastructure, which is a meaningful gap. The seniority ask (Principal) is also above Riley's stated target ceiling.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM and conversational product overlap, but Principal-level seniority and backend/developer platform focus are stretches beyond Riley's target archetype and stated seniority ceiling.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered and conversational AI experiences at scale — including an on-site Copilot-style chat product at Samsung.com (10M+ monthly users) and an automated knowledge-extraction pipeline that drove 8x AI agent traffic. Proven track record applying contextual data, behavioral signals, and RAG-adjacent architectures to reduce customer friction and drive measurable business outcomes.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata (product attributes, inventory, pricing) from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%
gap_handling: The biggest gap is the backend/developer platform focus — Twilio serves developers and wants someone who has built internal AI infrastructure for technical personas. Riley should explicitly frame the AEO pipeline and recommendation API work as backend service ownership, and call out any experience with API design, data pipelines, or developer-facing products. Downplaying "e-commerce" framing and leading with "AI orchestration" and "knowledge pipeline" language will help bridge this gap.

title: Staff Product Manager, AI Personalization
score_rationale: Riley's core strengths — hybrid AI search, LLM-driven pipelines, personalization systems, A/B experimentation, and cross-functional leadership at scale — map closely to what this role demands technically and strategically. The gap is that this is a Staff-level Platform PM role anchored in customer support AI (a domain Riley has no direct experience in), with heavy emphasis on RAG/retrieval architecture and full-stack platform ownership across multiple surfaces — a step up in scope and seniority from Riley's current PM III role at Samsung. The conversational AI and AEO pipeline work at Samsung is the strongest bridge, but the customer support / CS platform domain is a meaningful mismatch from Riley's e-commerce discovery background.
recommendation_reason: Strong technical and AI personalization fit, but Staff seniority is a stretch and CS platform domain is a notable departure from Riley's e-commerce search background — worth applying with targeted framing.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years building AI-powered personalization and search systems at consumer scale — including hybrid search, LLM-driven pipelines, conversational AI, and recommendation platforms serving 10M+ users. Proven track record partnering with ML engineers on retrieval architecture, experimentation design, and multi-surface product strategy that drives measurable engagement and conversion outcomes.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience — from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch — in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data; launched add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%
Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
gap_handling: The CS platform / customer support AI domain is new territory — address this head-on in a cover letter by drawing parallels: your conversational AI work at Samsung (discovery & comparison flows) is functionally similar to support AI (resolution flows), and your full-stack ownership of search ranking + personalization systems demonstrates the platform-thinking this role requires. Avoid overstating RAG experience — reference semantic search, retrieval optimization, and LLM pipeline ownership as honest entry points into that conversation.

title: Senior Product Manager
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product chops, A/B testing discipline, and conversational AI experience (Samsung chatbot) map well to the Help Site's AI-powered self-service direction. However, the core of this role — redesigning a knowledge base / Help Center platform, support deflection metrics, and CustOps agent tooling — is a domain Riley has no direct resume evidence for, which is a notable gap. The Growth context and stakeholder management experience are a solid match, but this role skews toward internal tooling and support enablement over Riley's primary archetype of search, discovery, and e-commerce.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI and Growth credentials partially offset the lack of Help Center / support product experience, but the core domain is a meaningful stretch — apply only with significant resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: Senior PM with 9+ years driving AI-powered discovery and personalization experiences at Samsung and Amazon, with a strong track record of launching conversational AI, self-service flows, and data-driven product redesigns that improve user success at scale. Proven Growth PM with deep experience using AI to reduce friction, increase conversion, and deliver measurable engagement improvements across complex cross-functional organizations.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience — from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch — supporting product discovery and comparison for 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com

Saved $1.4M in operational cost by enhancing self-serve workflow for 2M+ B2B sellers at Amazon, reducing manual support contacts through targeted UX fixes validated by 10+ interviews and 300+ seller surveys
Defined on-site search product vision and roadmap at Samsung.com, leading phased rollout with A/B testing that increased web navigation CSAT/NPS by 20% QoQ across key conversion funnels
gap_handling: Riley has no explicit Help Center or support-platform PM experience. To close this gap in the application, frame the Samsung conversational AI chatbot as a self-service deflection product, highlight the Amazon seller self-serve cost-savings story as support enablement, and add a brief cover letter note acknowledging the platform redesign scope and connecting it to Riley's e-commerce discovery overhauls.

title: Staff Product Manager, Lyft AI Platform & Marketplace Applications
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — hybrid search, LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and experimentation at scale — that align well with Lyft's AI platform mandate. However, this role is explicitly a Staff PM focused on an internal AI Platform (agentic systems, multi-agent orchestration, platform evangelism, build-vs-buy decisions, and SaaS vendor management), which is an archetype Riley hasn't held directly; Riley's background is predominantly consumer-facing AI product, not internal developer/platform PM. Additionally, the role is a stretch seniority-wise (Staff PM vs. Riley's target of Senior PM), and the San Francisco hybrid requirement introduces a location friction point for a Bellevue-based candidate.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML signal and technical depth are compelling, but the Platform PM archetype, Staff-level seniority, SF hybrid requirement, and agentic/multi-agent focus represent meaningful gaps that require deliberate resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping end-to-end AI/ML systems — from hybrid search and LLM-powered pipelines to conversational AI and recommendation platforms — across Samsung, Amazon, and high-growth e-commerce. Proven ability to partner with data scientists and engineers to define AI platform strategy, instrument KPIs at scale, and drive measurable adoption across 10M+ users and 2M+ B2B sellers.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks — demonstrating end-to-end LLM pipeline ownership.

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines across 20+ categories.

Defined success metrics for search engagement and led instrumentation strategy in partnership with engineering and analytics teams — directly mapping to Lyft's KPI tracking and data visualization requirements.
gap_handling: The biggest gap is Platform PM experience: Riley has built AI-powered consumer products but hasn't been the PM of an internal AI platform serving internal teams as customers. To bridge this, frame the AEO pipeline and recommendation API work as internal platform delivery, and explicitly call out cross-team evangelism moments (e.g., partnering with AI researchers, data scientists across orgs). On agentic/multi-agent orchestration, only reference it if Riley has genuine hands-on familiarity — don't overclaim, but do highlight the conversational AI and LLM pipeline work as adjacent evidence.

recommendation_reason: Strong AI product credentials and sponsorship match, but the required engineering background and dev tools domain are real gaps that make this a stretch application.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — including conversational AI, agentic pipelines, and model-driven search — at Samsung and Amazon. top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines

Consider adding a "Technical Projects" section to the resume if any coding work exists.

title: Product Manager, Consumer

score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — hybrid search, LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and measurable consumer-facing impact — that align well with Anthropic's Consumer team mission. The technical depth signal (e.g., prototyping with Claude Code) and the founder/startup archetype are meaningful gaps that could be disqualifying for top-tier candidates at this level.

suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years launching consumer-facing AI experiences — from hybrid search and conversational AI to personalized recommendation systems serving 10M+ monthly users. Known for translating emerging ML capabilities into shipped products with measurable impact, with a hands-on approach to prototyping, experimentation, and 0-to-1 product creation across consumer and e-commerce domains.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA

The resume shows AOV and conversion wins, but those were driven by personalization and discovery mechanics, not dedicated monetization strategy. The 6+ years requirement and seniority match well, and Anthropic is an elite AI brand with visa sponsorship, making this a credible stretch worth tailoring for.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI domain credibility and analytical chops, but the role demands deep monetization / subscription growth ownership that Riley's resume doesn't foreground — requires significant tailoring to surface relevant conversion and revenue signals.

Experienced in A/B testing, funnel optimization, and cross-functional execution; passionate about building safe, high-impact AI products that serve millions of users.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing

title: Senior Product Manager - Data Agent
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product chops, SQL fluency, LLM/conversational AI experience, and A/B experimentation rigor map well to Datadog's "AI-powered query experience" and governance layer. However, the core hard requirement — domain expertise in BI, semantic layers, data warehouses, and the modern data stack — is largely absent from Riley's resume, which focuses on e-commerce search/discovery rather than analytics tooling or enterprise data infrastructure. Additionally, Datadog explicitly wants B2B SaaS product experience at scale (observability/DevTools), and while Riley has one B2B stint at Amazon, it's seller tooling rather than analytics. The 0-to-1 entrepreneurial framing is also a stretch for a candidate whose resume emphasizes iterative optimization over greenfield product creation.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM and SQL signals, but missing the BI/data stack domain knowledge and B2B SaaS depth this 0-to-1 role critically requires.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years building LLM-powered experiences, conversational AI, and data-driven discovery systems at scale — including an on-site conversational AI chat, behavioral signal-based ranking, and structured metadata pipelines. Excited to apply this AI product depth to Datadog's 0-to-1 Data Agent, bringing a track record of cross-functional 0→1 launches, governance-minded instrumentation strategy, and strong SQL/analytics fluency to serve both data teams and business consumers.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks
Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system using multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines, reducing manual listing time by 80%
gap_handling: Be direct about the BI/data stack gap: acknowledge you haven't shipped semantic layers or BI tooling, but position your conversational AI chat, LLM eval exposure, AEO metadata pipeline, and CDP work as the closest analogs. In your cover letter or screening, show you've done homework — reference tools like dbt, Looker, Databricks, or Hex — and frame your e-commerce governance/instrumentation experience as a proxy for data team workflows.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
location: Bellevue, Washington; Chicago, Illinois; New York, New York; Washington, DC
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — shipping LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and search systems — which covers the "AI-adjacent" half of this role well. However, the core requirement is deep Identity & Access Management expertise (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust, agent authentication/authorization), a domain Riley has no evident background in. This is a developer/security-platform PM role, not a consumer AI or e-commerce discovery role, so the archetype fit is meaningful but partial. The AEO pipeline and API design experience (recommendation API, structured data) hint at technical depth, but IAM is a hard, specialized requirement that can't easily be papered over.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product credibility covers ~half the role, but zero IAM/OAuth/OIDC/security experience is a significant gap for the core requirement of this position.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI and hybrid search to structured data pipelines and recommendation APIs — at Samsung and Amazon scale. Experienced in defining technical protocols, partnering with engineers on system design, and launching "zero-to-one" AI products for millions of users; eager to apply this foundation to securing the next generation of AI agent identities at Okta.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Developed a personalized recommendation API with data scientists using product, browsing, and purchase data, leveraged it to launch an add-ons page with UX designers and engineers, boosting AOV by 15%
gap_handling: Be upfront in your cover letter: acknowledge you're coming from the AI product side rather than the IAM/security side, and position yourself as the "AI-native PM who understands agentic systems from the inside." Consider quickly studying OAuth2/OIDC flows and Zero Trust basics (Auth0 docs, Okta developer blog) so you can speak credibly to the protocol layer in an interview. If you get a screen, lean hard into the AEO pipeline (AI agent traffic) and conversational AI work — those are the closest signals to "building for agents."

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents

However, this role is squarely in enterprise identity security (IAM, OAuth2/OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust), a domain Riley has no documented experience in, which is a hard requirement, not a nice-to-have. Additionally, the role is developer-platform / B2B enterprise, a departure from Riley's consumer/e-commerce core. The LLM and AI agent awareness partially bridges the gap, but the identity infrastructure expertise is a meaningful missing piece.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM product background is genuinely relevant, but the IAM/OAuth/OIDC domain expertise is a hard requirement Riley can't currently satisfy — needs honest gap acknowledgment.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered pipelines, conversational AI, and multi-agent workflows at Samsung and Amazon, reaching 10M+ monthly users. gap_handling: The biggest gap is IAM domain knowledge (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust). Before applying, Riley should invest a few hours in self-study — read Okta's developer docs on OAuth2/OIDC flows, skim the emerging MCP (Model Context Protocol) spec, and reference any API design or token-based auth work (even indirectly) in the resume. In the cover letter, frame the AEO pipeline and multi-agent workflow familiarity as a direct bridge to AI agent governance, and be upfront that the identity security domain is a learning edge — Okta may value the fresh AI product instincts over a traditional IAM background for this zero-to-one charter.

title: Senior Product Manager, Project & Task Experience
score_rationale: Riley's core PM strengths — cross-functional leadership, data-driven roadmapping, and A/B experimentation — are solid matches for Asana's execution expectations, and her seniority level aligns cleanly. However, the role is firmly rooted in B2B enterprise SaaS, complex UX/data modeling for a work-management platform, and project/task workflow design — domains Riley has little direct experience in. Her background is deeply commerce-side (search, discovery, e-commerce, seller tools), and Asana's JD explicitly favors "complex data models and visualizations, human-centric UX, and B2B platform or enterprise experience" — areas where Riley's resume offers only partial overlap (Amazon B2B seller tools being the closest proxy). The AI curiosity signal in the JD is a plus for Riley, but it's a nice-to-have, not the core of the role.
recommendation_reason: Riley meets the seniority bar and has transferable PM fundamentals, but the B2B enterprise SaaS / work-management domain is a meaningful stretch from her e-commerce AI search core; the NYC office requirement also adds friction.
suggested_summary: Senior Product Manager with 9 years of experience building data-driven, cross-functional product experiences at scale — including B2B seller platform tools at Amazon and AI-powered discovery systems at Samsung serving 10M+ users monthly. Proven track record of defining roadmaps for complex, ambiguous problem spaces, partnering tightly with engineering, design, and data science to ship high-velocity, high-impact products.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines across 20+ categories.

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI, improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users and boosting NPS by 20% QoQ.

Saved $1.4M in operational cost by enhancing a self-serve workflow for B2B sellers — identifying root-cause issues via 10+ interviews and 300+ surveys, then coordinating across 5 dependent teams to deliver a targeted solution.
gap_handling: The biggest gap is enterprise SaaS / work-management domain knowledge — Asana builds productivity tooling for teams, not commerce or search. In your cover letter and interviews, lean hard into your Amazon B2B platform experience (data pipelines, seller workflows, multi-team coordination) as your closest analog to complex enterprise product work. Acknowledge the domain shift proactively and frame it as a feature: you bring fresh, user-centric AI thinking from high-stakes e-commerce environments that most Asana-native PMs lack.
